history.reasoning:
The input data (throughput measurements) are X = {312.4, 298.7, 415.2, 389.6, 301.1}. N = 5.

1. Calculate the Arithmetic Mean (Mean):
Sum of values (ΣX) = 312.4 + 298.7 + 415.2 + 389.6 + 301.1 = 1717.0
Mean (μ) = ΣX / N = 1717.0 / 5 = 343.40

2. Calculate the Sample Standard Deviation (s):
First, calculate the squared differences from the mean:
(312.4 - 343.4)² = (-31.0)² = 961.00
(298.7 - 343.4)² = (-44.7)² = 1998.09
(415.2 - 343.4)² = (71.8)² = 5155.24
(389.6 - 343.4)² = (46.2)² = 2134.44
(301.1 - 343.4)² = (-42.3)² = 1789.29

Sum of squared differences (Σ(X-μ)²): 961.00 + 1998.09 + 5155.24 + 2134.44 + 1789.29 = 12038.06

Calculate the Sample Variance (s²):
N - 1 = 5 - 1 = 4
Variance (s²) = 12038.06 / 4 = 3009.515

Calculate the Sample Standard Deviation (s):
Standard Deviation (s) = sqrt(3009.515) = 54.8500

Rounding the results to 2 decimal places gives Mean = 343.40 and Standard Deviation = 54.85.
